Full Guide

14-Day South China Karst Nature Route

Guilin - Yangshuo - Longji - Libo - Huangguoshu

This handbook is written directly for international visitors. It is not a general sightseeing introduction, but an actionable 14-day nature route guide. Visitors can use it to choose accommodation areas, plan daily sightseeing, connect transport segments, select meals, estimate budgets and handle backup plans. All prices are reference prices only. Final costs should be checked again according to travel dates on official platforms, Trip.com, Ctrip, Qunar, Dianping, hotel front desks or scenic-area ticket counters.

Start full guideSee basic info
Planning Basics

Basic Information

Designed for first-time international visitors. Use this section to confirm route theme, duration, entry and exit cities, seasons, difficulty and budget assumptions.

Basic Information Details
Route Theme Karst mountains and rivers, Li River peak forests, Yulong River countryside, Longji Rice Terraces, Libo emerald-water forest, Huangguoshu Waterfall and Longgong underground river
Recommended Duration 14 days / 13 nights
Route Entry / Exit Enter from Guilin; leave from Guiyang
Suitable For First-time international visitors to China, nature photographers, families, couples and light-hiking travelers
Best Seasons April-June and September-November; summer has stronger water flow but is humid, while winter is quieter but less colorful
Difficulty Moderate; no high-altitude sickness risk, but includes mountain roads, scenic shuttles, long-distance transfers and some walking
Exchange Rate Used USD 1 approximately RMB 7.2. USD amounts are rounded and for budgeting only.
Route Highlights

Core scenic experiences

Guilin’s Iconic Karst Mountains and Rivers
Peaceful Bamboo Rafting on the Yulong River
Panoramic Longji Rice Terraces
Emerald Waters of Libo Xiaoqikong and Daqikong
The Majestic Huangguoshu Waterfall
A Green Nature Escape at Qianling Mountain Park
Guide Sections
1. Route Logic and City / Scenic Area Introduction2. 14-Day Route Overview3. Core Price Reference Table (RMB + USD)4. Accommodation Guide: Location, Foreign-Visitor Convenience and Reasons to Stay5. Dining Guide: Rating References, Specialties and Reasons to Choose6. Intercity Transport and Price Table7. Day-by-Day Detailed Travel Guide8. Three-Level Budget Estimate (14 Days, Excluding International Flights)9. Practical Tips for International Visitors10. Risks and Backup Plans

1. Route Logic and City / Scenic Area Introduction

This route links together the most representative karst landscapes of South China. Guilin and Yangshuo present open river-and-peak-forest scenery. Longji Rice Terraces add mountain and seasonal agricultural landscapes. Libo presents forested water scenery and World Heritage karst. Huangguoshu and Longgong complete the route with waterfalls and underground rivers. The route focuses on natural scenery rather than shopping, nightlife or cultural ancient-town tourism.

Place City / Scenic Area Introduction Accommodation and Travel Advice
Guilin One of China’s most classic landscape cities and a suitable first stop for international visitors entering the South China karst route. City sightseeing is relatively easy, with many hotel and transport choices. Stay near Two Rivers and Four Lakes, Elephant Trunk Hill or Zhengyang Pedestrian Street for easy arrival and first-night recovery.
Yangshuo The endpoint of the Li River cruise and the most suitable slow-travel town on this route. Yulong River, Ten-Mile Gallery, Xianggong Mountain and Xingping are all nearby. Stay in the Yulong River area if scenery is the priority; stay on the edge of West Street for dining convenience, but avoid the bar-street core.
Longji Rice Terraces Mountain terrace scenery suitable for sunrise, clouds and seasonal rice-field colors. May-June is best for mirror-like flooded fields; September-October is best for golden rice fields. Ping’an Village is more developed; Dazhai/Jinkeng is better for photography. Do not bring large suitcases uphill.
Libo Xiaoqikong, Daqikong and Maolan form a combination of forest, water, canyon and karst landscapes. Xiaoqikong has the most beautiful water color on this route. Stay near the east or west gate of Xiaoqikong for early entry; stay in Libo County for easier dining.
Huangguoshu / Anshun Huangguoshu is a major waterfall scenic area with the Main Waterfall, Doupotang and Tianxing Bridge sections. Anshun functions as the hotel and transport hub. Stay near Huangguoshu for early entry; stay in downtown Anshun if dining and city convenience are more important.
Guiyang The capital of Guizhou and the best final-night buffer city. Hotels, airport access, rail stations and dining options are more complete. Stay near the airport for early flights, near Guiyang North Station for high-speed rail, or downtown for a later departure.

2. 14-Day Route Overview

Day Route Main Experience Overnight
Day 1 Arrive in Guilin Arrival, rest, payment and network check Guilin
Day 2 Guilin city natural sights Reed Flute Cave, Elephant Trunk Hill, optional Two Rivers and Four Lakes / Seven Star Park Guilin
Day 3 Guilin - Li River Cruise - Yangshuo Li River cruise, karst peak forests, arrival in Yangshuo Yangshuo
Day 4 Yulong River in Yangshuo Bamboo rafting, Gongnong Bridge, Ten-Mile Gallery, Moon Hill Yangshuo
Day 5 Flexible Yangshuo nature day Xianggong Mountain, Xingping Li River, Ruyi Peak, Silver Cave or rest Yangshuo
Day 6 Yangshuo - Longji Rice Terraces Long mountain-road transfer and terrace sunset Longji
Day 7 Full day in Longji Sunrise, viewpoints and terrace walking Longji
Day 8 Longji - Libo Long transfer and arrival rest Libo
Day 9 Libo Xiaoqikong Wolong Pool, Water Forest, waterfalls and Xiaoqikong Bridge Libo
Day 10 Daqikong / Maolan / Rest Canyon, natural bridge, forest karst or recovery day Libo
Day 11 Libo - Huangguoshu / Anshun Transfer and preparation for waterfall day Huangguoshu / Anshun
Day 12 Full day at Huangguoshu Waterfall Main Waterfall, Doupotang and Tianxing Bridge Huangguoshu / Anshun
Day 13 Longgong / Anshun Rest / Transfer to Guiyang Cave underground river or easy transfer Guiyang / Anshun
Day 14 Depart from Guiyang Departure day, no distant scenic area -

3. Core Price Reference Table (RMB + USD)

Area Attraction / Item RMB USD Notes
Guilin Reed Flute Cave RMB 82-90 USD 11-13 1-1.5 hours; cave scenery, suitable for rain and easy sightseeing
Guilin Elephant Trunk Hill Free Free Reservation may be required; city landmark, suitable for easy photos
Guilin Two Rivers and Four Lakes Night Cruise RMB 180-220 USD 25-31 Optional; cancel if tired on arrival day
Guilin - Yangshuo 3-star Li River Cruise RMB 215 USD 30 Budget option; onboard food is simple
Guilin - Yangshuo 4-star Li River Cruise RMB 360 USD 50 More recommended for international visitors; better seats and comfort
Yangshuo Yulong River Bamboo Raft RMB 180-320 / raft USD 25-44 Different piers have different prices; book through official piers
Yangshuo Xianggong Mountain RMB 60-80 USD 8-11 Sunrise and high viewpoint over the Li River; cancel in poor weather
Yangshuo Silver Cave From RMB 95 From USD 13 Rainy-day cave alternative
Yangshuo Ruyi Peak RMB 200-300 USD 28-42 Cable car / skywalk; not ideal for visitors afraid of heights
Longji Longji Rice Terraces Entrance RMB 80 USD 11 Local shuttle or luggage help may be extra
Longji Scenic Shuttle / Local Transport RMB 40-80 USD 6-11 Depends on village and transfer method
Libo Xiaoqikong Entrance RMB 100-120 USD 14-17 Season-dependent; check before travel
Libo Xiaoqikong Scenic Shuttle RMB 40 USD 6 Highly recommended because the site is large
Libo Daqikong RMB 50-80 USD 7-11 Depends on same-day combo tickets or platform prices
Libo Maolan RMB 50-100 USD 7-14 Advanced outdoor route; confirm open areas
Huangguoshu Huangguoshu Ticket + Shuttle RMB 210 USD 29 Core scenic-area ticket and shuttle recommended for most visitors
Anshun Longgong Scenic Area RMB 130-170 USD 18-24 Depends on whether boat, shuttle and elevator are included
Guiyang Qianling Mountain / Easy City Visit RMB 5-60 USD 1-8 Optional before departure; avoid distant sights on departure day
Price note: Scenic tickets, cruises and shuttle prices may vary by season, holidays, platform bundles and temporary scenic-area policies. Use this table for route budgeting and itinerary design, and check again on official platforms, Trip.com, Ctrip, Qunar or scenic-area counters before departure.

4. Accommodation Guide: Location, Foreign-Visitor Convenience and Reasons to Stay

Destination Recommended Stay Location Why Recommended Foreign-Visitor Convenience Reference Average Price
Guilin Shangri-La Guilin Qixing / north side of the Li River, suitable for families and higher budgets Close to the Li River; international brand; more stable foreign-guest reception High RMB 900-1,800 / USD 125-250
Guilin Sheraton Guilin Hotel Downtown riverside location near Zhengyang Pedestrian Street Convenient for walking and taxis; suitable for first-arrival visitors High RMB 700-1,500 / USD 97-208
Guilin Comfort hotels around Two Rivers and Four Lakes / Elephant Hill Central lake area or around Elephant Hill Convenient for Days 1-2. For Atour/JI/Elong-style hotels, confirm passport check-in before booking. Medium-High RMB 350-800 / USD 49-111
Yangshuo Banyan Tree Yangshuo Fuli Town / high-end riverside resort area Good for couples, families and high-budget travelers; quiet but farther from the county center High RMB 1,800-4,000 / USD 250-556
Yangshuo Yangshuo Sugar House Design-hotel area by the Li River Strong design style; suitable for high-end photography and resort travelers High RMB 1,500-3,800 / USD 208-528
Yangshuo Yangshuo Mountain Retreat Yulong River area Close to Yulong River and countryside scenery; highly aligned with this nature route Medium-High RMB 600-1,200 / USD 83-167
Yangshuo Comfort hotels outside the West Street core Outer West Street area, not the bar-street center Convenient dining, shops and taxis; quieter than the core nightlife area Medium-High RMB 350-900 / USD 49-125
Longji Longji One / Qingshan No.1 Courtyard type stays Dazhai / Jinkeng scenic area Good terrace views; suitable for photography. Confirm uphill luggage arrangements before arrival. Confirm before booking RMB 600-1,500 / USD 83-208
Longji Cloud / panoramic guesthouse type stays Higher areas of Dazhai / Tiantouzhai Close to viewpoints and sunrise locations, but many steps; not suitable for large suitcases Confirm before booking RMB 400-1,200 / USD 56-167
Longji Ping’an Village scenic guesthouses More developed Ping’an Village area Clear routes and more manageable walking; suitable for first-time Longji visitors Confirm before booking RMB 250-700 / USD 35-97
Libo Libo Xiaoqikong Junlan Resort Hotel Near Xiaoqikong scenic gate Good for early entry and fewer backtracks; more expensive than county hotels Medium-High RMB 700-1,500 / USD 97-208
Libo Yungexuan / Yishan guesthouse type stays Near Xiaoqikong East Gate / ticket hall Convenient for next-day entry; confirm foreign-passport registration before booking Confirm before booking RMB 300-900 / USD 42-125
Libo Comfort hotels in Libo County Central Libo County Better access to restaurants, shops and taxis; good for budget or comfort travelers Medium RMB 250-700 / USD 35-97
Huangguoshu Bolian Resort Huangguoshu Near Huangguoshu scenic area High-end scenic stay; good for early entry and higher budgets Medium-High RMB 900-2,500 / USD 125-347
Huangguoshu Huangguoshu Hotel Near the scenic area Convenient for early entry on waterfall day Medium-High RMB 400-1,000 / USD 56-139
Anshun DoubleTree by Hilton Anshun Downtown Anshun More complete city facilities and more stable foreign-guest service High RMB 500-1,200 / USD 69-167
Guiyang Kempinski / Sofitel / Novotel and similar hotels Downtown Guiyang, Guiyang North Station or airport area Best for the final buffer night; international/chain hotels are more reliable High RMB 500-1,500 / USD 69-208
Accommodation note: International visitors usually need their original passport to check in at hotels in China. International chains, higher-star hotels and properties bookable through major platforms usually handle foreign guests more smoothly. Mountain guesthouses, scenic-area inns and smaller-town stays should be contacted before payment to confirm whether passport check-in is accepted. “Confirm before booking” does not mean the hotel is not recommended; it means the visitor should confirm again through Trip.com, Ctrip customer service or the hotel before paying.

5. Dining Guide: Rating References, Specialties and Reasons to Choose

Place Restaurant / Food Type Platform Rating Reference Specialty Why Recommended Average Cost
Guilin Chunji Roasted Goose Approx. 4.3-4.7 Roasted goose, Guangxi dishes, group dinner Stable environment and broad menu, suitable as the first proper dinner after arrival RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21
Guilin Chongshan Rice Noodles / Lao Dongjiang type noodle shops Approx. 4.0-4.5 Guilin rice noodles, breakfast / quick meal Low-cost and fast; good before morning sightseeing or transfers RMB 10-25 / USD 1-4
Guilin Xiao Nan Guo / local Guangxi restaurants Approx. 4.1-4.5 Guangxi home-style dishes Stable flavor; suitable for international visitors who do not want very spicy food RMB 60-120 / USD 8-17
Yangshuo Master Beer Fish Approx. 4.2-4.7 Yangshuo beer fish High tourist recognition; good first try of the local specialty RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21
Yangshuo Huoxiaoniu Private Kitchen Beer Fish Approx. 4.3-4.7 Beer fish and local hot dishes Near the West Street area, suitable for visitors staying in town or on the West Street edge RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21
Yangshuo Mitang Shengmingyue Riverside Restaurant Approx. 4.3-4.7 Riverside dining, local dishes Good for visitors who want atmosphere and photo-friendly dining RMB 120-250 / USD 17-35
Yangshuo Hotel restaurants / Banyan Tree Ming Yue / Sugar House dining Approx. 4.3+ High-end Chinese / Western / design-hotel dining Good for higher-budget travelers, families or visitors who do not want to go into town at night RMB 200+ / USD 28+
Longji Longji local restaurants / guesthouse meals Approx. 4.0-4.6 Bamboo rice, mountain bamboo shoots with cured pork, bamboo chicken Most practical in the mountain area, especially before or after sunrise RMB 50-120 / USD 7-17
Longji Qingshan No.1 Terrace View Restaurant Approx. 4.2-4.7 Terrace-view dining, local food Eat while overlooking the terraces; suitable for photography and scenic dining RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21
Libo Centennial Kaili Sour Fish Soup near Xiaoqikong East Gate Approx. 4.1-4.6 Sour fish soup, Guizhou cuisine Convenient dinner for visitors staying near Xiaoqikong RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21
Libo Libo Four Special Dishes Approx. 4.1-4.6 Local specialty dishes Good for visitors who want to try local food and share dishes as a group RMB 60-120 / USD 8-17
Libo Bean hotpot / ecological beef restaurants Approx. 4.0-4.5 Bean hotpot, beef dishes Stable dinner choice, good for sharing RMB 60-120 / USD 8-17
Huangguoshu Bolian Hotel Waterfall-view Restaurant Approx. 4.3+ High-end Guizhou / hotel dining Suitable for higher-budget visitors or those staying at scenic hotels RMB 150+ / USD 21+
Huangguoshu Buyi Eight Bowls Ethnic Cuisine Approx. 4.1-4.6 Buyi ethnic flavors, Guizhou cuisine Good for groups, families and visitors wanting ethnic local flavors RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21
Anshun / Guiyang Qianren Baiwei / Lao Kaili Sour Soup Fish / beef noodles Approx. 4.0-4.6 Guizhou cuisine, sour soup fish, beef noodles More choices in the city; suitable as a stable final meal before departure RMB 30-150 / USD 4-21
Rating note: Restaurant ratings are reference ranges commonly seen on public platforms such as Dianping, Ctrip and Trip.com. Specific branches, seasons and platform standards vary. Before departure, search the Chinese name on Dianping, Trip.com or Ctrip again to verify operating status, ratings and queue conditions.

6. Intercity Transport and Price Table

Segment Best Option Time Reference Price Suitable For Notes
Guilin Airport -> Guilin hotel Taxi / hotel pickup 40-60 min RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21 All visitors Do not use complex transfers on arrival day; save the hotel Chinese name
Guilin rail stations -> city hotel Taxi 20-50 min RMB 25-80 / USD 3-11 All visitors Do not confuse Guilin Station, Guilin North and Guilin West
Guilin hotel -> Li River cruise pier Hotel transfer / private car 40-70 min RMB 80-200 / USD 11-28 Day 3 cruise visitors Confirm the Chinese pier name and meeting time
Guilin -> Yangshuo Li River cruise 4-5 hrs RMB 215-360 / USD 30-50 First-time Guilin visitors Both transport and core attraction
Guilin -> Yangshuo Private car / expressway 1.5-2 hrs RMB 350-600 / USD 49-83 Visitors short on time / rainy day Faster but without Li River experience
Yangshuo -> Longji Private car 4-5.5 hrs RMB 700-1,200 / USD 97-167 International visitors, families, luggage travelers Most recommended; reduces transfer risk
Yangshuo -> Guilin -> Longji Shared car / tourist bus 5-7 hrs RMB 120-250 / USD 17-35 Budget travelers More complex; not recommended for first-time China visitors
Longji -> Libo Direct private car 6.5-8 hrs RMB 1,500-2,500 / USD 208-347 Families, small groups, higher budgets Use the day only for transfer
Longji -> Guilin -> Libo Private car + high-speed rail/train + local transfer 7-9 hrs RMB 300-800 / USD 42-111 Budget travelers Need to check date-specific schedules and stations
Libo -> Huangguoshu / Anshun Private car 4.5-6 hrs RMB 1,000-1,800 / USD 139-250 Families and comfort travelers Fewer transfers, suitable with luggage
Libo -> Anshun / Guiyang Rail/train combination 5-7 hrs RMB 200-500 / USD 28-69 Budget travelers Confirm station names and taxi distance after arrival
Anshun -> Huangguoshu Taxi / private car 45-75 min RMB 120-250 / USD 17-35 Visitors staying in Anshun Leave early for early entry
Anshun / Huangguoshu -> Longgong Taxi / private car 1-1.5 hrs RMB 150-300 / USD 21-42 Optional Day 13 visitors Depends on hotel location and onward transfer
Anshun -> Guiyang High-speed rail / private car 30 min-2 hrs RMB 50-500 / USD 7-69 All visitors Stay near Guiyang airport/rail station on the last night
Guiyang city -> airport Taxi / metro / hotel drop-off 30-60 min RMB 40-120 / USD 6-17 Departure visitors Arrive 2.5-3 hours early for international flights

7. Day-by-Day Detailed Travel Guide

Day 1 | Arrival in Guilin

Item Details
Today’s Focus Arrival, hotel check-in, payment and network check. Do not schedule intensive sightseeing.
Route Departure city -> Guilin city
Overnight Guilin
Estimated Cost RMB 450-1,800 / USD 63-250 (including accommodation, local transport and dinner)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

After arrival | Airport / rail station to hotel (suggested time: 40-60 minutes). Take a taxi or pre-book hotel pickup. The goal is a smooth check-in, not saving money through complex transfers.

16:00-18:00 | Hotel rest and payment check (1-2 hours). Confirm Alipay, WeChat Pay, foreign bank card, cash, translation app and mobile data.

18:30-20:00 | Easy dinner (1-1.5 hours). Choose Guilin rice noodles, Chunji Roasted Goose or a nearby hotel restaurant.

20:00-21:00 | Optional short walk around Two Rivers and Four Lakes (30-60 minutes). Keep it short and close to the hotel.

Transport and Distance Notes

Guilin Liangjiang Airport to downtown usually takes 40-60 minutes and costs about RMB 80-150 (USD 11-21).

Guilin has several railway stations. Confirm whether you arrive at Guilin Station, Guilin North or Guilin West, as taxi time and cost differ significantly.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Dinner: Chunji Roasted Goose (RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21) or Guilin rice noodles (RMB 10-25 / USD 1-4).

If you just arrived and are not yet comfortable with local food, choose a hotel or mall restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Stay near Two Rivers and Four Lakes, Elephant Trunk Hill or Zhengyang Pedestrian Street. First-time visitors should prioritize international brands or major-platform hotels for smoother passport check-in.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Save the hotel Chinese name, address and phone number.

Do not overfill the arrival day.

For late-night arrivals, use hotel pickup or official taxis.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

If your flight is delayed, cancel all evening activities and go directly to the hotel.

Day 2 | Guilin City Natural Sights

Item Details
Today’s Focus A light introduction to Guilin caves and city landscape.
Route Guilin city
Overnight Guilin
Estimated Cost RMB 650-1,800 / USD 90-250 (including accommodation, tickets, meals and taxis)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-09:30 | Breakfast: Guilin rice noodles or hotel breakfast (30-60 minutes). Keep it simple before visiting Reed Flute Cave.

09:30-11:30 | Reed Flute Cave (1-1.5 hours). A classic Guilin cave with stalactites, stalagmites and lighting. Ticket reference: RMB 82-90 / USD 11-13.

11:30-13:00 | Return downtown for lunch (1-1.5 hours). Choose local Guangxi dishes and avoid overexertion.

14:00-16:00 | Elephant Trunk Hill (1-2 hours). A Guilin landmark, suitable for easy photos. Usually free but may require reservation.

16:00-17:30 | Two Rivers and Four Lakes lakeside walk or hotel rest (about 1 hour). Return to the hotel if hot or tired.

18:00-20:00 | Dinner and Day 3 cruise confirmation (1.5 hours). Confirm pier, meeting time, luggage and pickup.

Transport and Distance Notes

Downtown Guilin to Reed Flute Cave by taxi takes about 20-30 minutes and costs around RMB 30-60 (USD 4-8).

From Reed Flute Cave to Elephant Trunk Hill, take a taxi or return to the hotel before going out again. Public-bus transfers are not recommended for international visitors.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch: Xiao Nan Guo / Guangxi cuisine (RMB 60-120 / USD 8-17).

Dinner: Chunji Roasted Goose (RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21) or a hotel restaurant (RMB 100-200 / USD 14-28).

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Continue staying in central Guilin for easier departure to the Li River pier on Day 3.

Foreign Visitor Notes

The cave floor can be slippery; wear non-slip shoes.

Elephant Trunk Hill may require advance reservation.

Tonight, confirm the Li River cruise pier in Chinese.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

In rain, keep Reed Flute Cave and cancel outdoor walking. If tired, visit only Reed Flute Cave and Elephant Trunk Hill.

Day 3 | Guilin - Li River Cruise - Yangshuo

Item Details
Today’s Focus Use the Li River cruise as the core scenic transfer from Guilin to Yangshuo.
Route Guilin -> Li River Cruise -> Yangshuo
Overnight Yangshuo
Estimated Cost RMB 1,000-3,500 / USD 139-486 (including hotel, cruise, meals and transfers)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

07:30-08:30 | Hotel breakfast and checkout (1 hour). Handle large luggage according to cruise or hotel instructions.

08:30-09:30 | Transfer to the Li River pier (40-70 minutes). The pier is outside the city center; book a cruise with pickup or arrange a private car.

09:30-14:00 | Li River Cruise (4-5 hours). A 4-star cruise is recommended (about RMB 360 / USD 50). Enjoy karst peak forests, river reflections and the Xingping section.

14:00-15:30 | Arrive in Yangshuo and go to the hotel (1-1.5 hours). Do not schedule intense activities after arrival.

16:00-18:00 | Hotel rest or short riverside walk (1-2 hours). Settle in and confirm Day 4 Yulong River plans.

18:30-20:00 | Dinner in Yangshuo (1.5 hours). Try beer fish or dine at the hotel.

Transport and Distance Notes

Guilin hotel to the cruise pier costs about RMB 80-200 (USD 11-28). A cruise ticket with pickup is easier.

3-star cruise: about RMB 215 (USD 30); 4-star cruise: about RMB 360 (USD 50). The 4-star option is better for international visitors and families.

If weather or water levels affect the cruise, take a private car or expressway transfer to Yangshuo, about 1.5-2 hours, RMB 350-600 (USD 49-83).

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Onboard lunch is usually simple; bring water and snacks.

Dinner: Master Beer Fish or Huoxiaoniu Beer Fish (RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21); higher-budget visitors can choose a hotel restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

For natural scenery, stay in the Yulong River area. For dining convenience, stay near but not inside the West Street nightlife core.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Carry passport and booking screenshots.

Confirm whether luggage travels with you or separately.

Do not search the pier at the last minute; use the Chinese pier name.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

If not taking the cruise, transfer by private car to Yangshuo and spend the afternoon resting at the hotel or walking on the edge of West Street.

Day 4 | Yulong River and Ten-Mile Gallery

Item Details
Today’s Focus Close-up experience of Yangshuo countryside, rivers and karst peaks.
Route Yangshuo town / Yulong River area
Overnight Yangshuo
Estimated Cost RMB 900-3,500 / USD 125-486 (including hotel, bamboo raft, private car and meals)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast and transfer to Yulong River pier (1 hour). Staying in the Yulong River area makes this easier; town stays require taxi/private car.

09:00-11:00 | Yulong River bamboo raft (40-90 minutes). Choose a classic route. Reference: RMB 180-320 per raft (USD 25-44). Waterproof your phone and passport.

11:00-12:00 | Gongnong Bridge / riverside photos (30-60 minutes). Good for river, peak and countryside reflections.

12:00-13:30 | Lunch (1-1.5 hours). Choose Yulong River farmhouse dishes or return to town.

14:00-16:30 | Ten-Mile Gallery and Moon Hill viewpoint (2-2.5 hours). A private car with photo stops is recommended. Long-distance e-bike riding is not recommended for visitors unfamiliar with Chinese roads.

17:00-18:00 | Hotel rest (1 hour). Avoid heat and overexposure.

18:30-20:00 | Dinner (1.5 hours). Choose beer fish, local dishes or a hotel restaurant.

Transport and Distance Notes

Yangshuo town to Yulong River piers by taxi/private car takes about 20-40 minutes and costs RMB 40-120 (USD 6-17).

Half-day private local transport costs about RMB 300-600 (USD 42-83), useful for families, older visitors and non-cyclists.

Bicycle rental is about RMB 20-50 (USD 3-7), e-bike rental about RMB 50-100 (USD 7-14). International visitors should prioritize safety.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch: Yulong River farmhouse food (RMB 50-120 / USD 7-17).

Dinner: Beer fish (RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21) or riverside dining (RMB 120-250 / USD 17-35).

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Continue staying in Yangshuo. If nature is the priority, choose the Yulong River area. If dinner convenience matters, stay on the West Street edge.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Use only official bamboo-raft piers.

Rafts may stop during heavy rain, high water or safety control.

Carry some cash in countryside areas.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

In rain, cancel bamboo rafting and visit Silver Cave or rest at the hotel. If low on energy, do a short raft route plus Gongnong Bridge only.

Day 5 | Flexible Yangshuo Nature Day

Item Details
Today’s Focus Choose high viewpoints, caves or rest according to weather and energy.
Route Yangshuo -> Xianggong Mountain / Xingping / Ruyi Peak / Silver Cave
Overnight Yangshuo
Estimated Cost RMB 800-3,800 / USD 111-528 (depending on private transport and hotel level)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

05:00-08:30 | Option A: Xianggong Mountain sunrise (2-3 hours). Recommended in good weather for Li River bends, peaks and morning mist. Do not wake early in rain. Ticket reference: RMB 60-80 / USD 8-11.

09:30-12:00 | Xingping Li River section (1.5-2 hours). Suitable for riverside walking and classic Li River photos. It can be crowded, so watch belongings.

12:00-13:30 | Lunch (1-1.5 hours). Simple meal in Xingping or return to Yangshuo.

14:00-16:30 | Option B/C: Ruyi Peak or Silver Cave (2-2.5 hours). Ruyi Peak is better in clear weather; Silver Cave is better on rainy days.

After 17:00 | Hotel rest / café / riverside walk (1-2 hours). Reduce intensity if the previous days were tiring.

Transport and Distance Notes

Yangshuo to Xianggong Mountain round-trip private car costs about RMB 300-600 (USD 42-83). Early departure must be arranged in advance.

Yangshuo to Xingping takes about 40-60 minutes and costs RMB 120-250 (USD 17-35) by taxi/private car.

Ruyi Peak or Silver Cave can be decided based on weather; half-day private car costs about RMB 300-600 (USD 42-83).

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch: Simple meal in Xingping (RMB 30-80 / USD 4-11) or return to Yangshuo town.

Dinner: Local restaurant or light meal near the West Street edge (RMB 80-200 / USD 11-28).

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Continue staying in Yangshuo. Rest early tonight before the long mountain-road transfer on Day 6.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Check weather before Xianggong Mountain; do not force a sunrise trip in rain or heavy fog.

Laozhai Mountain is not the default option for ordinary visitors.

Pack and organize luggage tonight for Day 6.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

In rain, choose Silver Cave. If tired, make this a rest day in Yangshuo.

Day 6 | Yangshuo - Longji Rice Terraces

Item Details
Today’s Focus Transfer from Yangshuo peak forests to mountain terraces; focus on travel and sunset.
Route Yangshuo -> Longji Rice Terraces
Overnight Longji
Estimated Cost RMB 900-3,000 / USD 125-417 (including hotel, private car, meals and ticket)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast and checkout (1 hour). Prepare a small overnight bag and confirm large-luggage arrangements with the driver/hotel.

09:00-14:00 | Yangshuo to Longji by road (4-5.5 hours). Private car is recommended. Rest stops can be arranged; do not add extra attractions.

14:00-15:30 | Enter scenic area and reach guesthouse (1-1.5 hours). Some guesthouses require walking or local transfer; contact the hotel in advance.

16:00-18:00 | Viewpoints near the guesthouse / sunset (1-2 hours). If arrival is late, do not walk mountain paths after dark.

18:30-20:00 | Guesthouse dinner (1.5 hours). This is the most convenient dining option in the mountains.

Transport and Distance Notes

Yangshuo to Longji private car costs about RMB 700-1,200 (USD 97-167) and takes 4-5.5 hours.

Shared tourist bus costs about RMB 120-250 (USD 17-35) but involves more transfers and communication.

Internal scenic transport may cost RMB 40-80 (USD 6-11), depending on village location.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch: roadside or simple meal (RMB 30-80 / USD 4-11).

Dinner: guesthouse meal (RMB 50-120 / USD 7-17); try bamboo rice, bamboo-shoot cured pork and bamboo chicken.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Ping’an Village suits first-time and lower-energy visitors. Dazhai/Jinkeng suits photographers and sunrise visitors. Confirm foreign-passport check-in, vehicle drop-off point and luggage help before booking.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Do not bring large suitcases uphill.

Ask the hotel in advance whether they can help carry luggage.

Mountain roads are winding; bring motion-sickness medicine if needed.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

If arriving late, just check in and rest. Cancel sunset viewing in rain.

Day 7 | Full Day in Longji Rice Terraces

Item Details
Today’s Focus Sunrise, terrace viewpoints and light walking.
Route Inside Longji Rice Terraces
Overnight Longji
Estimated Cost RMB 550-1,900 / USD 76-264 (including hotel, meals and local scenic transport)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

05:30-07:30 | Terrace sunrise (1-2 hours). Choose Golden Buddha Peak, West Hill Music, Seven Stars with Moon or Nine Dragons and Five Tigers according to where you stay.

07:30-09:00 | Breakfast and rest (1-1.5 hours). Return to the guesthouse after sunrise.

09:30-12:00 | Easy or standard terrace walk (2-2.5 hours). Easy version: nearest viewpoint only. Standard version: connect 2-3 viewpoints.

12:00-13:30 | Guesthouse lunch (1-1.5 hours). Eating at or near the guesthouse is easiest.

14:00-16:30 | Afternoon viewpoint or rest (1-2.5 hours). Visit another viewpoint for different light, or rest at the guesthouse.

18:00-20:00 | Dinner and Day 8 transport confirmation (1.5 hours). Confirm driver, departure time and Libo hotel address.

Transport and Distance Notes

Inside Longji, travel is mainly on foot, by shuttle or with guesthouse assistance. Some viewpoints can be reached by cableway or local transport.

Dazhai/Jinkeng has broader views but longer distances; Ping’an Village has more developed paths.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Breakfast: guesthouse breakfast (usually RMB 20-50 / USD 3-7).

Lunch/dinner: guesthouse or local restaurant (RMB 50-120 / USD 7-17 per person).

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Stay in the same guesthouse for two nights to avoid moving luggage.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Wear non-slip shoes; stone paths and field edges are slippery after rain.

Do not step into terraces or damage field ridges.

English service is limited; save the Chinese address.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

In rain, walk only a short viewpoint route. If tired, choose one viewpoint only.

Day 8 | Longji - Libo

Item Details
Today’s Focus Long transfer day; the goal is to reach Libo safely.
Route Longji -> Libo
Overnight Libo
Estimated Cost RMB 1,000-3,600 / USD 139-500 (depending on transport and hotel level)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

07:30-08:30 | Breakfast and checkout (1 hour). Settle bills and confirm luggage down the mountain.

08:30-16:30 | Longji to Libo transfer (6.5-8 hours). Recommended: direct private car, or Longji-Guilin-rail/train-Libo combination. No sightseeing today.

16:30-18:00 | Arrive in Libo and check in (1-1.5 hours). Confirm Xiaoqikong entry gate, tickets and shuttle for Day 9.

18:30-20:00 | Dinner in Libo (1.5 hours). Recommended: sour fish soup, Libo local dishes or bean hotpot.

Transport and Distance Notes

Direct private car costs about RMB 1,500-2,500 (USD 208-347), suitable for families and small groups.

Rail/train combination costs about RMB 300-800 (USD 42-111), but transfers are complicated and schedules must be checked for the exact date.

Do not schedule any attraction on Day 8, as mountain roads, traffic or transfers may cause delays.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Dinner: Kaili-style sour fish soup or Libo local dishes (RMB 60-150 / USD 8-21 per person).

If arriving late, choose a simple meal near the hotel.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Stay near Xiaoqikong East/West Gate for early entry, or in Libo County for easier dining. Confirm passport check-in before booking.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Save the Libo hotel Chinese address.

Confirm Xiaoqikong ticket, entry gate and shuttle tonight.

Avoid long evening activities.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

If the transfer is exhausting, start Day 9 later or use the light Xiaoqikong route.

Day 9 | Full Day at Libo Xiaoqikong

Item Details
Today’s Focus Emerald water, waterfalls, Water Forest and ancient bridge - the most beautiful water scenery on the route.
Route Libo -> Xiaoqikong Scenic Area -> Libo
Overnight Libo
Estimated Cost RMB 700-2,000 / USD 97-278 (including hotel, ticket, shuttle, meals and taxi)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

07:30-08:30 | Breakfast and transfer to scenic entrance (1 hour). Enter early to avoid group-tour peaks.

08:30-10:00 | Wolong Pool (1-1.5 hours). One of the best places for water-color photos; visit in the morning.

10:00-11:30 | Yuanyang Lake / Cuigu Waterfall area (1-1.5 hours). Add water activities only if you have energy.

11:30-13:00 | Simple scenic-area lunch or packed food (about 1 hour). Food options are limited; bring water and snacks.

13:00-15:30 | Water Forest and 68-level waterfalls (2-2.5 hours). Strong experience but slippery; wear non-slip shoes.

15:30-16:30 | Xiaoqikong Ancient Bridge (30-60 minutes). The symbol of the area; expect crowds and be patient for photos.

17:00-18:00 | Return to hotel and rest (1 hour). Avoid a packed evening after a walking-heavy day.

Transport and Distance Notes

Libo County to Xiaoqikong entrance by taxi/private car takes 30-60 minutes and costs around RMB 60-200 (USD 8-28).

Xiaoqikong ticket: RMB 100-120 (USD 14-17); scenic shuttle about RMB 40 (USD 6). The shuttle is strongly recommended.

Use scenic shuttle buses between key points rather than walking the entire site.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Breakfast: hotel breakfast.

Lunch: simple scenic meal or packed light food (RMB 30-80 / USD 4-11).

Dinner: sour fish soup, bean hotpot or ecological beef (RMB 60-150 / USD 8-21).

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Continue staying in Libo. If visiting Daqikong next day, staying near the scenic area is convenient; if dining matters more, stay in the county.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Carry your passport in case of real-name ticket checks.

Bring a waterproof phone bag and non-slip shoes.

The scenic area is large; check shuttle directions carefully.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

In rain, water color can still be beautiful, but reduce Water Forest walking. If tired, visit only Wolong Pool, Xiaoqikong Bridge and the waterfall highlights.

Day 10 | Daqikong / Maolan / Libo Rest Day

Item Details
Today’s Focus Choose a lighter canyon, advanced forest route or rest according to energy.
Route Around Libo
Overnight Libo
Estimated Cost RMB 600-2,000 / USD 83-278 (depending on Maolan and transport method)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-09:30 | Breakfast and decide the day’s option (1 hour). Base the choice on Day 9 energy level.

09:30-13:00 | Option A: Daqikong (2-4 hours). Default recommendation: canyon, natural bridge and river scenery; easier than Xiaoqikong.

09:00-16:00 | Option B: Maolan (5-7 hours). Suitable for light-hiking and forest exploration visitors. Requires private car and open-area confirmation.

Morning/afternoon | Option C: Libo rest (half day or full day). Good for families, older visitors or anyone tired from Day 9. Sort photos, eat in town and restock supplies.

18:00-20:00 | Dinner and Day 11 transport confirmation (1.5 hours). Confirm transport to Huangguoshu/Anshun, hotel address and departure time.

Transport and Distance Notes

Taxi/private car from Libo County/Xiaoqikong to Daqikong costs about RMB 60-200 (USD 8-28).

Maolan is best done by private car; half-day/full-day reference: RMB 400-800 (USD 56-111). Confirm route in advance.

If Day 11 transport is early, choose accommodation that is convenient for departure.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch: simple meal near Daqikong or return to town (RMB 30-80 / USD 4-11).

Dinner: Libo local dishes, ecological beef or bean hotpot (RMB 60-120 / USD 8-17).

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Continue staying in Libo. If leaving by private car, location is flexible; if using rail/train combination, stay in the county for easier taxi access.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Ordinary visitors should choose Daqikong by default rather than going to Maolan spontaneously.

English service in Maolan is limited; arrange driver and route in advance.

Do not walk muddy forest trails in rain.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

If Day 9 was tiring, rest. In rain, choose Daqikong or rest in town.

Day 11 | Libo - Huangguoshu / Anshun

Item Details
Today’s Focus Transfer from Libo to the waterfall region; use the day only for transport and preparation.
Route Libo -> Huangguoshu / Anshun
Overnight Huangguoshu / Anshun
Estimated Cost RMB 900-3,500 / USD 125-486 (depending on accommodation and transport level)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast and checkout (1 hour). Confirm luggage, tickets or driver.

09:00-15:00 | Libo to Huangguoshu/Anshun (4.5-6.5 hours). Private car is recommended; budget travelers can use rail/train combinations.

15:00-16:30 | Check in (about 1 hour). If staying near Huangguoshu, confirm the next-day entrance. If staying in Anshun, confirm the morning car to the scenic area.

16:30-18:00 | Rest and prepare gear (about 1 hour). Prepare raincoat, waterproof bag, non-slip shoes, passport and ticket order.

18:30-20:00 | Dinner (1.5 hours). Eat local food near Huangguoshu or choose from more options in Anshun city.

Transport and Distance Notes

Libo to Huangguoshu/Anshun by private car costs around RMB 1,000-1,800 (USD 139-250) and takes about 4.5-6 hours.

Rail/train combination costs around RMB 200-500 (USD 28-69), but you must confirm station names, schedules and taxi distance.

Anshun downtown to Huangguoshu scenic area the next morning takes 45-75 minutes and costs about RMB 120-250 (USD 17-35).

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Dinner: Buyi Eight Bowls, Yun-Gui restaurants, Qianren Baiwei or hotel restaurants (RMB 60-180 / USD 8-25 per person).

Higher-budget visitors can choose the Bolian Huangguoshu hotel restaurant (RMB 150+ / USD 21+).

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Stay near Huangguoshu for early entry; stay in downtown Anshun for dining convenience. Because Day 12 is intensive, choose a hotel with convenient transport and reliable breakfast.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Do not schedule evening activities.

Buy Huangguoshu ticket + shuttle in advance.

Prepare a raincoat, not just an umbrella.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

If transfer is delayed, check in and rest; choose a hotel or nearby restaurant for dinner.

Day 12 | Full Day at Huangguoshu Waterfall

Item Details
Today’s Focus The natural climax of the whole route: Main Waterfall, Doupotang and Tianxing Bridge.
Route Huangguoshu Scenic Area
Overnight Huangguoshu / Anshun
Estimated Cost RMB 900-2,800 / USD 125-389 (including hotel, ticket, shuttle, meals and transport)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

07:00-08:00 | Breakfast and transfer to scenic area (1 hour). If staying in Anshun, leave earlier. Scenic-area hotels make early entry easier.

08:00-10:30 | Huangguoshu Main Waterfall (2-2.5 hours). Visit the core waterfall first. In high-water season, expect strong mist.

10:30-11:30 | Water Curtain Cave if open (30-60 minutes). Opening depends on water volume, safety and maintenance; check on site.

11:30-12:30 | Lunch / supplies (1 hour). Scenic-area simple meal or packed food.

12:30-14:00 | Doupotang Waterfall (1-1.5 hours). Easier route and wide waterfall views.

14:00-17:00 | Tianxing Bridge (2-3 hours). A karst walkway of stone, water, trees and caves. If tired, walk only the highlights.

17:00-18:00 | Return to hotel (1 hour). Rest in the evening after heavy walking.

Transport and Distance Notes

Huangguoshu ticket + shuttle reference: RMB 210 (USD 29). The scenic area is large, so use shuttles efficiently.

Round-trip transport from Anshun to Huangguoshu costs about RMB 240-500 (USD 33-69).

Do not board the wrong internal shuttle; show Chinese attraction names to staff if needed.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch: simple scenic-area meal or packed food (RMB 30-80 / USD 4-11).

Dinner: Huangguoshu Hotel restaurant, Buyi Eight Bowls or Guizhou food in Anshun (RMB 80-180 / USD 11-25).

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Continue staying near Huangguoshu or in Anshun. If going to Longgong on Day 13, downtown Anshun is more convenient.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Wear non-slip shoes, not flip-flops.

Keep phone, passport and camera in a waterproof bag.

Do not combine this day with a flight or long rail transfer.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

For older visitors or families, visit only Main Waterfall and Doupotang. Add full Tianxing Bridge only if energy is good.

Day 13 | Longgong / Anshun Rest / Transfer to Guiyang

Item Details
Today’s Focus Buffer day after Huangguoshu: choose underground-river cave or go directly to Guiyang.
Route Anshun / Huangguoshu -> Longgong -> Guiyang
Overnight Guiyang / Anshun
Estimated Cost RMB 700-2,500 / USD 97-347 (depending on Longgong and hotel level)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast and energy check (1 hour). Choose the plan according to Day 12 fatigue.

09:30-13:00 | Option A: Longgong Scenic Area (2-4 hours). Suitable for visitors who like caves, underground rivers and boat-in-cave experiences. Ticket reference: RMB 130-170 / USD 18-24.

Morning/afternoon | Option B: Supplementary Huangguoshu visit (2-4 hours). For visitors who did not complete Tianxing Bridge or want more waterfall photos.

12:00-15:00 | Option C: Rest and transfer to Guiyang (half day). The safest option to reduce Day 14 departure risk.

16:00-18:00 | Arrive in Guiyang and check in (1-2 hours). Strongly recommended if you have an early flight or train next day.

18:30-20:00 | Dinner in Guiyang (1.5 hours). Try sour fish soup, beef noodles or a mall restaurant.

Transport and Distance Notes

Anshun/Huangguoshu to Longgong by taxi/private car takes 1-1.5 hours and costs RMB 150-300 (USD 21-42).

Anshun to Guiyang by high-speed rail starts from about 30 minutes, usually RMB 50-100 (USD 7-14); private car takes 1.5-2 hours and costs RMB 400-600 (USD 56-83).

If departing early on Day 14, arrive in Guiyang this afternoon.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch: Longgong simple meal or Anshun restaurant (RMB 60-150 / USD 8-21).

Dinner: Guiyang sour fish soup, beef noodles or mall restaurant (RMB 30-150 / USD 4-21).

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

For the final night, stay near Guiyang airport, Guiyang North Station or downtown. Choose airport-area hotels for early flights and North Station hotels for high-speed rail.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Longgong cave paths are slippery, and boat routes may be affected by water levels.

If Day 12 was exhausting, prioritize rest and transfer to Guiyang.

Do not schedule a distant attraction before departure day.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

If tired, cancel Longgong. For early flights, stay near Guiyang airport.

Day 14 | Depart from Guiyang

Item Details
Today’s Focus Departure day; only light city activity or direct departure.
Route Guiyang -> next destination
Overnight -
Estimated Cost RMB 100-600 / USD 14-83 (excluding long-distance flight/rail ticket)

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

Morning | Hotel breakfast and packing (1 hour). Check passport, flight/rail ticket, payment tools and luggage.

Depending on departure time | Optional light city activity (1-2 hours). If departure is late, walk nearby, eat in a mall or visit a nearby park. Do not visit distant attractions.

Before departure | Transfer to airport / rail station (30-60 minutes). Arrive at the airport 2.5-3 hours before international flights; arrive at rail station 1-1.5 hours before departure.

Transport and Distance Notes

Downtown Guiyang to Longdongbao Airport by taxi costs about RMB 40-120 (USD 6-17) and takes 30-60 minutes.

Guiyang has several railway stations. Confirm Guiyang North, Guiyang East or Guiyang Railway Station before leaving.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Breakfast: hotel breakfast.

Lunch/dinner: mall restaurant, airport or rail-station simple meal according to departure time (RMB 40-150 / USD 6-21).

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

No planned accommodation. If your flight is changed or delayed, choose a chain hotel near the airport or railway station.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Do not schedule distant scenic areas on departure day.

Check the Chinese name of your airport or station in advance.

Allow extra time for traffic, security checks and passport-related processes.

Rainy-Day / Low-Energy Alternative

If departure is delayed, stay near the airport or rail station.

8. Three-Level Budget Estimate (14 Days, Excluding International Flights)

Level Configuration Total RMB Budget Total USD Budget Suitable For and Notes
Budget Regular hotels/guesthouses, some shared cars/rail, simple meals RMB 9,500-13,500 USD 1,319-1,875 Young travelers or visitors with China independent-travel experience; more transfers and lower comfort
Comfort (Recommended) Comfort hotels + private cars for key segments + local restaurants RMB 16,000-24,000 USD 2,222-3,333 Best for most international visitors; balanced transport and accommodation experience
High-End High-end resort/view hotels + mostly private cars + selected restaurants RMB 32,000-55,000 USD 4,444-7,639 Families, couples and higher-budget visitors; reduces communication and transfer pressure
Category Budget Comfort High-End
Accommodation RMB 4,000-7,000 / USD 556-972 RMB 8,000-14,000 / USD 1,111-1,944 RMB 20,000-38,000 / USD 2,778-5,278
Intercity Transport / Private Cars RMB 2,000-3,500 / USD 278-486 RMB 4,000-7,000 / USD 556-972 RMB 8,000-12,000 / USD 1,111-1,667
Tickets / Scenic Shuttles RMB 1,800-2,800 / USD 250-389 RMB 2,200-3,200 / USD 306-444 RMB 3,000-4,500 / USD 417-625
Dining RMB 1,700-3,000 / USD 236-417 RMB 3,000-5,500 / USD 417-764 RMB 6,000-10,000 / USD 833-1,389
Backup Cash / Miscellaneous RMB 500-1,200 / USD 69-167 RMB 1,000-2,000 / USD 139-278 RMB 2,000-4,000 / USD 278-556
Budget note: These are per-person reference budgets, excluding international flights, shopping and travel insurance. If two travelers share hotels and private cars, the per-person accommodation and transport cost may decrease. During Chinese public holidays, summer peak season or high-end hotel sell-out periods, accommodation and private-car prices may rise significantly.

9. Practical Tips for International Visitors

Documents and Ticketing

Carry your original passport with you; do not rely only on photos. Hotel check-in, high-speed rail, scenic-area ticket windows and real-name reservation checks may require the physical passport.

If an online ticketing page does not accept passports, try Trip.com, Ctrip international version, hotel front desk assistance or the scenic-area manual ticket counter.

During peak periods, confirm tickets at least 1-3 days in advance, especially for Huangguoshu, Xiaoqikong and the Li River cruise.

Payment and Cash

Use Alipay as the main payment tool and WeChat Pay as a backup. Carry Visa/Mastercard and RMB 500-1,500 in cash. Mountain areas, villages, small stalls and drivers may rely more on cash.

If mobile payment fails, try switching between Alipay and WeChat first, then use cash or ask your hotel for assistance.

Language and Navigation

Save all hotel, attraction, station and airport names in Chinese. Showing drivers Chinese addresses is more reliable than English addresses.

Prepare useful Chinese sentences such as: “Please take me to this hotel,” “I need to buy a ticket with my passport,” “I need the manual ticket counter,” and “Please help me call a car.”

Luggage and Gear

Longji Rice Terraces are not suitable for dragging large suitcases uphill. Prepare a small two-night overnight bag. Essential items include non-slip shoes, raincoat, waterproof phone bag, power bank, mosquito repellent, motion-sickness medicine and stomach medicine.

Huangguoshu and Xiaoqikong have strong moisture and water spray. A raincoat is more practical than an umbrella.

10. Risks and Backup Plans

Risk Possible Impact Backup Plan
Rain Yulong River rafts, Xianggong sunrise, Longji walking, Xiaoqikong Water Forest and Huangguoshu Tianxing Bridge may be affected Yangshuo: Silver Cave or hotel rest; Longji: short viewpoint walk; Xiaoqikong: reduce Water Forest walking; Huangguoshu: keep Main Waterfall and Doupotang
Low Energy Fatigue after many consecutive walking and transfer days Skip Moon Hill in Yangshuo; choose one viewpoint in Longji; use Xiaoqikong shuttle; visit only Main Waterfall + Doupotang; cancel Longgong on Day 13
Transport Delay Most likely on Day 8 Longji-Libo and Day 11 Libo-Huangguoshu Use these days only for transfer; stay in Guiyang on the final night; do not combine major attractions with flights
Ticketing Failure Online system may not accept passports; scenic-area quotas may fill up on holidays Use Trip.com/Ctrip international version, ask hotel front desk, go to manual ticket window or switch to a backup attraction
Payment Failure Foreign-card binding fails, weak mountain network, small merchants do not support mobile payment Prepare four options: Alipay, WeChat, bank card and cash. Pay key transport arrangements in advance where possible.
14-Day South China Karst Nature Route · Full Guide · Designed for first-time international visitors
